name: 15_Image_Captioning_ShowAndTell_Flickr8k_DL
query: This is my current PyTorch project: Develop an automatic image captioning system using the Show and Tell model. Here I found a repo can guide you: https://github.com/sgrvinod/a-PyTorch-Tutorial-to-Image-Captioning. Use the dataset Flickr8k dataset, downloading it from [this link](https://huggingface.co/datasets/jxie/flickr8k) and load it in `src/data_loader.py`. The system should generate descriptions of sample images and save them to `results/metrics/generated_descriptions.txt`. An attention mechanism must be implemented in `src/model.py`. Save the pre-trained model as `models/saved_models/show_and_tell_model.pt`. Visualize the attention weights and save it to `results/figures/attention_weights.png`. The dataset should load smoothly, with proper error handling if any issues arise. Ideally, the attention mechanism should clearly highlight the image regions that are most influential in generating captions.

requirements.criteria: The pre-trained "Show and Tell" model is used.
The "Flickr8k" dataset, potentially downloaded from [this link](https://huggingface.co/datasets/jxie/flickr8k), is loaded in `src/data_loader.py`.
The attention mechanism is implemented in `src/model.py`.
Generated descriptions of sample images are saved in `results/metrics/generated_descriptions.txt`.
The trained model is saved as `models/saved_models/show_and_tell_model.pt`.
A visualization of attention weights is saved as `results/figures/attention_weights.png`.

preferences.criteria: The dataset should load smoothly, with clear error handling if any issues arise during download.
The attention mechanism should clearly highlight the image regions that contribute most to the generated captions.

judge_stats.criteria: The pre-trained "Show and Tell" model is used.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: The provided code does not show use of a pre-trained Show and Tell model. In `src/model.py`, `build_model()` returns only `"model placeholder"` and contains no model loading logic such as `torch.load(...)` or use of `models/saved_models/show_and_tell_model.pt`. The trajectory evidence also states there are no recorded executions or file operations involving the pre-trained Show and Tell model.

judge_stats.criteria: The "Flickr8k" dataset, potentially downloaded from [this link](https://huggingface.co/datasets/jxie/flickr8k), is loaded in `src/data_loader.py`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED> `src/data_loader.py` does not actually load the Flickr8k dataset. The file only defines placeholder metadata (`REQUIREMENT_CRITERIA`, `KEYWORDS = ["Flickr8k"]`) and a stub `load_dataset()` that returns `{"dataset": "loaded", "notes": requirement_notes()}`. There is no import or use of `datasets`, no file path handling for Flickr8k images/captions, and no code showing download or loading from the referenced Hugging Face dataset.

judge_stats.criteria: The attention mechanism is implemented in `src/model.py`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: `src/model.py` does not implement any attention mechanism. The file only contains a placeholder setup with `REQUIREMENT_CRITERIA`, `KEYWORDS = []`, a `requirement_notes()` function, and `build_model()` returning `"model placeholder"` (or a string about ResNet-18 if keywords matched). There is no attention class, layer, score computation, or forward-pass logic in the code.

judge_stats.criteria: Generated descriptions of sample images are saved in `results/metrics/generated_descriptions.txt`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED> `results/metrics/generated_descriptions.txt` exists, but its content is only `('placeholder\r\n', None)`, which is not evidence of actual generated image descriptions. The provided trajectory also contains no save/execution evidence confirming real sample-image captions were written to this file.

judge_stats.criteria: The trained model is saved as `models/saved_models/show_and_tell_model.pt`.

reason: <SATISFIED>: The workspace tree explicitly shows the file `models/saved_models/show_and_tell_model.pt`, and the file content evidence confirms “The file exists.” This directly satisfies the requirement that the trained model is saved at `models/saved_models/show_and_tell_model.pt`.

judge_stats.criteria: A visualization of attention weights is saved as `results/figures/attention_weights.png`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: Although a file exists at `results/figures/attention_weights.png` in the workspace tree, its provided content is not a valid image; it contains an error message: `"The image data you provided does not represent a valid image"` and `"Error reading image file"`. There is also no trajectory or execution evidence confirming successful generation of an attention-weight visualization.
